directive.title: RBAC part 2: RFQ route authorization using users.role

auditor.summary: The build implements the core authorization logic correctly with proper role-method mapping and test coverage for all 15 combinations. However, it has two CRITICAL issues: (1) uses `as any` type assertion violating the strict TypeScript requirement, and (2) inserts audit logs into the wrong table structure (auditLogs with payload JSON instead of authorization_audit_log with proper columns). Additionally, the mock session extraction in route handlers is a security concern if deployed, and the test suite is missing verification that no db.insert() calls occur on allowed requests. The build is recoverable but requires fixes before shipping.

top_issues.description: Uses `as any` type assertion on line 47: `allowedMethods.includes(method as any)`. This violates AC7 which requires 'no use of `any` type in the two created/modified auth files'.

Audit log insert uses auditLogs table but the spec requires authorization_audit_log table. The auditLogs table schema has entity_type, entity_id, action, payload columns - the code stores user_id, role, http_method, resource inside the payload JSON field rather than as top-level columns. This violates the spec requirement for direct column-level fields.
Test does not verify that zero db.insert() calls occur on allowed requests. AC5 explicitly requires this verification.
